Title: Order a coded drug from the concept dictionary with structured dosing
Preconditions: Clinician logged in with active visit; drug concept "Amoxicillin 500mg capsule" exists in dictionary
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Start order entry from active visit | 3. Search drug "Amoxicillin" | 4. Select coded formulation | 5. Set dose 500, units mg, frequency TID, route oral, duration 7 days | 6. Save order
Test_Data: Drug=Amoxicillin 500mg capsule; Dose=500mg; Freq=TID; Route=PO; Duration=7d
Expected_Result: Order saved as coded drug order with structured dose, units, frequency, route, duration; appears in active orders list with NEW status

Title: Create a free-text (non-coded) drug order when formulation not in dictionary
Preconditions: Clinician logged in with active visit; free-text ordering enabled in order entry config
Test_Steps: 1. Start order entry | 2. Choose free-text drug option | 3. Enter "Compounded magic mouthwash" | 4. Enter free-text dosing instructions | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Free-text order is saved with drugNonCoded populated and concept drug null; flagged visually as non-coded; included in active orders

Title: Reject free-text drug order when free-text ordering is disabled by config
Preconditions: Order entry configured to disallow free-text drug orders
Test_Steps: 1. Start order entry | 2. Attempt to enter a free-text drug name | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Free-text entry field is hidden or save is blocked with a validation message requiring a coded drug; no order persisted

Title: Boundary check dose at minimum allowed value (lower edge)
Preconditions: Drug with configured dose range exists; clinician with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Set dose to the minimum boundary value | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Order accepts the minimum valid dose without error; persisted dose equals entered value

Title: Reject zero dose value
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Set dose to 0 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error: dose must be greater than zero; order is not saved

Title: Reject negative dose value
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Enter dose -50 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error: negative dose rejected; order not persisted

Title: Boundary check very large dose triggers overdose warning
Preconditions: Drug with max safe dose configured; overdose check enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Set dose far above configured maximum | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System raises overdose/high-dose warning requiring override justification before saving; order not silently accepted

Title: Pairwise combination of dose units and routes
Preconditions: Drug supports multiple units and routes
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug for each pair below | 2. Save each | 3. Verify persisted values
Test_Data: Pairs: mg+PO; mL+IV; mcg+SC; units+IM; tablet+PO
Expected_Result: Each valid unit/route pair saves correctly with the chosen combination; mismatched/implausible combinations (e.g., tablet+IV) are flagged

Title: Order with complex frequency mapping (e.g., every 6 hours)
Preconditions: Frequency concepts configured including QID and Q6H
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Select frequency "Every 6 hours" | 3. Save | 4. Inspect order
Expected_Result: Frequency persisted with correct coded frequency concept and frequencyPerDay derived as 4

Title: Reject duration unit missing when duration value provided
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Enter duration value 7 | 3. Leave duration units blank | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error requiring duration units when a duration value is entered; order not saved

Title: Route required for parenteral drugs
Preconditions: Drug configured requiring explicit route
Test_Steps: 1. Order injectable drug | 2. Leave route blank | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation requires a route before saving; order rejected without route

Title: Order a PRN (as-needed) drug with reason
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit; PRN dosing supported
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Mark order as PRN | 3. Enter PRN reason "for pain" | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Order saved with asNeeded=true and asNeededCondition populated; PRN indicator visible in order list

Title: Require PRN reason when as-needed flag is set
Preconditions: PRN configured to require a reason
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug | 2. Mark PRN | 3. Leave PRN reason blank | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation requires PRN reason; order not saved until reason provided

Title: Order a tapering regimen with multiple sequential dose steps
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit; ability to create linked sequential orders
Test_Steps: 1. Create initial prednisone order at high dose | 2. Create subsequent linked orders at decreasing doses with staged start dates | 3. Save sequence
Test_Data: Drug=Prednisone; Steps=40mg x3d, 20mg x3d, 10mg x3d, 5mg x3d
Expected_Result: Sequential taper orders are created with non-overlapping scheduled dates and decreasing doses; regimen viewable as a coherent schedule

Title: Detect overlapping taper steps as scheduling conflict
Preconditions: Existing taper sequence on patient
Test_Steps: 1. Create taper step | 2. Create next step with start date before prior step ends | 3. Save
Test_Data: Step1=40mg start D0 end D3; Step2=20mg start D1
Expected_Result: System flags overlapping dosing schedule for the same drug as a potential duplicate/conflict warning

Title: Discontinue an active drug order with reason
Preconditions: Active drug order exists in NEW/active state
Test_Steps: 1. Open active orders | 2. Select active order | 3. Choose Discontinue | 4. Enter discontinue reason | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Order transitions active->discontinued; a DC order action recorded with reason; original order shown as stopped with stop date

Title: Cannot discontinue an already-discontinued order
Preconditions: Drug order already in discontinued state
Test_Steps: 1. Open the discontinued order | 2. Attempt Discontinue again
Expected_Result: Discontinue action unavailable or rejected; no duplicate DC action created

Title: Revise an active order changing dose creates new revision linked to prior
Preconditions: Active drug order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Select active order | 2. Choose Revise | 3. Change dose 500->250mg | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A REVISE order is created referencing previousOrder; original is stopped; new active order carries 250mg; chain auditable

Title: Reject revise that changes the drug concept (must discontinue+new)
Preconditions: Active drug order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Select active order | 2. Choose Revise | 3. Attempt to change the drug to a different concept | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Revise of the drug concept is blocked; user is directed to discontinue and place a new order instead

Title: Full lifecycle NEW->active->revised->discontinued state transitions
Preconditions: Clinician with active visit; order entry available
Test_Steps: 1. Create order (NEW) | 2. Confirm active | 3. Revise dose | 4. Discontinue | 5. Inspect order history
Expected_Result: Each transition recorded with timestamps, providers, and reasons; order history shows complete chronological chain with no orphan states

Title: Backdated stop date cannot precede order start date
Preconditions: Active drug order with known start date
Test_Steps: 1. Discontinue order | 2. Set stop date before the order start date | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error: stop date must not precede start date; order not stopped with invalid date

Title: Pharmacist performs generic-for-brand substitution during dispensing
Preconditions: Pharmacy/dispensing module enabled; brand order awaiting dispense; substitution policy permits generic
Test_Steps: 1. Open dispensing queue | 2. Select brand order | 3. Choose substitute generic equivalent | 4. Record substitution reason | 5. Dispense
Expected_Result: Substitution recorded with substituted drug, reason, and link to original order; dispense reflects substituted product; original prescriber intent preserved in record

Title: Block substitution when prescriber marked Dispense As Written
Preconditions: Order flagged DAW (no substitution); dispensing module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open DAW-flagged order in dispense queue | 2. Attempt generic substitution
Expected_Result: Substitution is blocked with message indicating Dispense As Written; only the ordered product may be dispensed

Title: Full dispense of an active drug order
Preconditions: Dispensing module enabled; active order with NEW dispense status
Test_Steps: 1. Open dispensing queue | 2. Select order | 3. Enter quantity equal to prescribed | 4. Confirm dispense
Expected_Result: Dispense recorded as complete; dispensed quantity equals prescribed; order dispense status set to completed; medication dispense event logged

Title: Partial dispense leaves remaining quantity outstanding
Preconditions: Dispensing module enabled; active order prescribed quantity 30
Test_Steps: 1. Open order in dispense queue | 2. Enter dispense quantity 10 of 30 | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Partial dispense recorded; remaining balance 20 tracked; order status partially-dispensed; subsequent dispense allowed up to remaining

Title: Reject cumulative dispense exceeding prescribed quantity
Preconditions: Order qty 30; 25 already dispensed
Test_Steps: 1. Open order | 2. Attempt to dispense 10 (total would be 35) | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Validation blocks over-dispense; cumulative dispensed cannot exceed prescribed quantity plus allowed refills; no record created

Title: Dispense against an authorized refill decrements refill count
Preconditions: Order with 3 authorized refills; initial fill done
Test_Steps: 1. Dispense refill | 2. Confirm | 3. Inspect remaining refills
Expected_Result: Refill recorded; remaining refills decremented to 2; dispense history shows refill sequence number

Title: Block dispense when no refills remain and order expired
Preconditions: Order with 0 refills remaining; original supply exhausted
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to dispense again | 2. Confirm
Expected_Result: Dispense blocked with message that refills are exhausted; a new prescription is required

Title: Boundary: refills count at zero and at configured maximum
Preconditions: Order entry supports refills field with max bound
Test_Steps: 1. Create order with refills=0 | 2. Create order with refills at configured max | 3. Attempt refills above max
Expected_Result: Zero and max refills accepted; refills above max rejected with validation

Title: Allergy alert fires when ordering a drug the patient is allergic to
Preconditions: Patient has recorded allergy to Penicillin; clinician ordering
Test_Steps: 1. Order Amoxicillin (penicillin class) | 2. Observe alert | 3. Acknowledge/override with reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy interaction alert is displayed before save citing the matched allergen; override requires documented reason; override is audited

Title: No allergy alert for unrelated drug with allergies on file
Preconditions: Patient allergic to Penicillin only
Test_Steps: 1. Order an unrelated drug (e.g., Lisinopril) | 2. Save
Expected_Result: No allergy alert raised; order saves normally; false-positive alerting avoided

Title: Override of allergy alert is captured in the audit log
Preconditions: Patient with recorded allergy; order triggering allergy alert
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger allergy alert | 2. Override with reason | 3. Save | 4. Inspect audit log
Expected_Result: Audit log records override event with user, timestamp, alert detail, and override reason; record is immutable

Title: Decision table for interaction severity outcomes
Preconditions: Interaction checking enabled with severity tiers
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug pairs per rules | 2. Observe alert behavior per severity | 3. Verify required action
Test_Data: R1 Contraindicated=hard stop; R2 Major=override+reason; R3 Moderate=warn+ack; R4 Minor=info only; R5 None=no alert
Expected_Result: Each severity tier produces the mandated UI behavior: contraindicated blocks, major requires justification, moderate requires acknowledgment, minor is informational, none is silent

Title: Contraindicated interaction hard-stops the order
Preconditions: Interaction list configured with a contraindicated pair
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug A | 2. Order contraindicated drug B | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Order is hard-stopped; save blocked entirely; clinician cannot override a contraindicated combination

Title: Major interaction allows override with mandatory reason
Preconditions: Interaction list with a major-severity pair
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug A | 2. Order interacting drug B | 3. Override with reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Major interaction alert shown; override permitted only with documented reason; both orders saved with override audited

Title: Duplicate therapy alert for same drug active twice
Preconditions: Active order for a drug exists
Test_Steps: 1. Order the same drug/ingredient again while first is active | 2. Observe alert
Expected_Result: Duplicate therapy warning raised citing the existing active order; clinician must confirm or cancel

Title: Therapeutic class duplication alert across different drugs same class
Preconditions: Active order for one NSAID exists
Test_Steps: 1. Order a second NSAID of the same therapeutic class | 2. Observe alert
Expected_Result: Therapeutic duplication warning fires for same-class agents; requires acknowledgment

Title: Weight-based pediatric dose range validation
Preconditions: Pediatric patient with weight recorded; weight-based dosing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Order weight-based drug | 2. Enter dose exceeding mg/kg max for patient weight | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System computes mg/kg and warns when above max per-kg dose; requires override; under-dose also flagged

Title: Renal-adjusted dosing alert for impaired renal function
Preconditions: Patient with low eGFR recorded; renal dosing rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Order renally-cleared drug at standard dose | 2. Observe renal alert
Expected_Result: Alert recommends renal dose adjustment given eGFR; clinician may accept recommended adjusted dose or override with reason

Title: Pregnancy-category warning when ordering for pregnant patient
Preconditions: Patient flagged pregnant; pregnancy contraindication data configured
Test_Steps: 1. Order a category-X drug | 2. Observe warning
Expected_Result: Pregnancy contraindication warning displayed; override requires explicit acknowledgment and reason; event audited

Title: Controlled substance order requires additional authorization/schedule capture
Preconditions: Drug flagged as controlled (e.g., Schedule II); user has prescribing privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Order controlled drug | 2. Provide required schedule/authorization data | 3. Save
Test_Data: Drug=Oxycodone Schedule II; Prescriber DEA captured
Expected_Result: Order requires controlled-substance metadata (schedule, prescriber authorization); missing data blocks save; action is heavily audited

Title: Reject controlled substance order by user lacking prescribing privilege
Preconditions: User without controlled-substance prescribing privilege logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to order a Schedule II drug | 2. Save
Expected_Result: Order is rejected with authorization error; no controlled order created; attempt logged

Title: Nurse cannot place a medication order without ordering privilege
Preconditions: User with nurse role lacking Add Orders privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Login as nurse | 2. Attempt to start drug order entry
Expected_Result: Order entry action is hidden or blocked; attempt to POST an order is rejected with 403; no order persisted

Title: Only pharmacist role may complete a dispense action
Preconditions: Clinician user without dispensing privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Login as clinician | 2. Open dispensing queue | 3. Attempt to dispense
Expected_Result: Dispense action blocked for non-pharmacist; API returns authorization error; dispense not recorded

Title: Create a MedicationRequest order via REST and verify persistence
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid session/token; patient and encounter exist
Test_Steps: 1. POST order payload to order endpoint | 2. Capture order uuid | 3. GET the order | 4. Validate fields
Test_Data: POST drugOrder JSON {drug, dose, frequency, route, encounter}
Expected_Result: API returns 201/200 with order uuid; GET returns identical structured dosing; order visible in UI active orders

Title: Reject order POST with missing required dose field
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST order payload omitting dose | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API responds 400 with validation error identifying missing dose; no order created

Title: Reject order POST without authentication token
Preconditions: REST API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST order payload with no/invalid auth header | 2. Inspect response
Test_Data: POST drugOrder with no Authorization header
Expected_Result: API responds 401 unauthorized; no order created; attempt logged

Title: Read a drug order as FHIR MedicationRequest resource
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; existing drug order
Test_Steps: 1. GET MedicationRequest by id | 2. Validate against R4 profile
Expected_Result: Resource returned with status, intent=order, medication[CodeableConcept/Reference], subject, dosageInstruction (dose, route, timing); validates against R4

Title: MedicationRequest medicationCodeableConcept carries correct coding system
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint; coded drug order using RxNorm or CIEL
Test_Steps: 1. GET MedicationRequest | 2. Inspect medicationCodeableConcept.coding.system and code
Expected_Result: Coding system URI is valid (e.g., RxNorm http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm) and code matches the ordered drug

Title: Create MedicationRequest via FHIR POST and verify dosageInstruction round-trip
Preconditions: FHIR R4 write enabled; valid token; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST MedicationRequest with dosageInstruction | 2. GET the created resource | 3. Compare dosage
Expected_Result: Created resource returns same dosageInstruction dose, route coding, and timing frequency; no data loss in round-trip

Title: MedicationDispense resource reflects partial dispense quantity
Preconditions: FHIR MedicationDispense supported; partial dispense performed
Test_Steps: 1. GET MedicationDispense for the order | 2. Inspect quantity and status
Expected_Result: MedicationDispense quantity=10, status reflects in-progress/partial, and authorizingPrescription links the MedicationRequest

Title: Outbound RDE (pharmacy order) message generated on order placement
Preconditions: HL7 v2 pharmacy interface configured; order placed
Test_Steps: 1. Place drug order | 2. Capture outbound HL7 message | 3. Parse segments
Expected_Result: An RDE^O11 message is emitted with ORC, RXO/RXE segments carrying drug, dose, route, frequency consistent with the order

Title: RDS dispense message reflects dispensed quantity
Preconditions: HL7 v2 dispense interface configured; dispense performed
Test_Steps: 1. Dispense order | 2. Capture outbound RDS message | 3. Parse RXD
Expected_Result: RDS^O13 message contains RXD segment with dispensed drug and quantity matching the dispense event

Title: Inbound malformed RXE segment is rejected with NAK
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send RDE message with malformed/truncated RXE | 2. Inspect ACK
Expected_Result: Interface responds with application reject (AE/AR) NAK; order not created from malformed message; error logged

Title: Discontinue creates new order row preserving original (no destructive update)
Preconditions: DB read access; active order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Note order_id of active order | 2. Discontinue via UI | 3. Query drug_order/orders tables
Test_Data: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE previous_order_id = original
Expected_Result: Original order row remains unchanged with stop date; a new discontinue order row references previous_order_id; immutable history preserved

Title: Voided order is flagged voided not physically deleted
Preconditions: DB read access; order voided via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Void an order | 2. Query orders table for the row
Test_Data: SELECT voided, void_reason FROM orders WHERE order_id=X
Expected_Result: Row persists with voided=true, void_reason, voided_by, date_voided populated; no physical delete; supports audit recovery

Title: Order number/uuid uniqueness constraint enforced
Preconditions: DB access; order creation
Test_Steps: 1. Create multiple orders | 2. Inspect order_number and uuid columns | 3. Attempt duplicate uuid insert
Expected_Result: Each order has unique order_number and uuid; duplicate uuid insert violates unique constraint and is rejected

Title: Order entry form is fully keyboard navigable
Preconditions: Order entry form open; keyboard only
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields | 2. Use Enter/Space to activate controls | 3. Submit via keyboard
Expected_Result: All fields, the drug search, and submit are reachable and operable by keyboard in logical order; visible focus indicators present

Title: Interaction/allergy alert is announced by screen reader
Preconditions: Screen reader active; allergy alert will trigger
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger allergy alert | 2. Listen for SR announcement
Expected_Result: Alert uses role=alert/aria-live so the warning text and severity are announced; override controls have accessible names

Title: Drug order dose field rejects script injection input
Preconditions: Order entry available
Test_Steps: 1. Enter script payload into free-text/instructions field | 2. Save | 3. Reopen order and view in lists
Expected_Result: Input is stored as data and rendered escaped (no script execution); no stored XSS in order views

Title: Clinician cannot order for a patient outside permitted location scope
Preconditions: Location-based access control configured; clinician scoped to Location A
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open/order for a patient assigned only to Location B
Expected_Result: Access to the patient and order entry is denied per location scoping; no order created; attempt audited

Title: Every order create/revise/discontinue is captured with provider and timestamp
Preconditions: Auditing enabled; clinician performing order actions
Test_Steps: 1. Create, revise, then discontinue an order | 2. Open audit/history view
Expected_Result: Each action logs actor, role, timestamp, action type, and before/after values; log entries are immutable and chronological

Title: Exploratory session on regimen edge cases and rapid order changes
Preconditions: Test patient; order entry available; tester charter defined
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly create/revise/discontinue same drug | 2. Try unusual unit/route combos | 3. Switch patients mid-entry | 4. Note anomalies
Expected_Result: No data corruption, stuck states, duplicate active orders, or wrong-patient persistence; anomalies documented with repro steps

Title: Wrong-patient safeguard when context switches during ordering
Preconditions: Two test patients; order entry started on patient A
Test_Steps: 1. Begin order on patient A | 2. Switch active patient to B before saving | 3. Save | 4. Verify which chart received order
Test_Data: Patient A then switch to B
Expected_Result: Order is associated with the intended patient only; system prevents silent wrong-patient assignment or warns on context change

Title: Continuous/indefinite order without duration is supported
Preconditions: Order entry supports continuous orders
Test_Steps: 1. Order a maintenance med | 2. Leave duration empty / mark continuous | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Order saved as ongoing/continuous with no stop date; appears in active orders indefinitely until discontinued

Title: Pairwise frequency vs duration-unit combinations
Preconditions: Frequencies and duration units configured
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug for each pair | 2. Save | 3. Verify computed schedule
Test_Data: Pairs: BID+days; QID+weeks; Q8H+days; weekly+weeks; once+hours
Expected_Result: Each frequency/duration-unit pair persists and computes a sensible schedule; implausible combos flagged

Title: Decision table for substitution permissions
Preconditions: Dispensing module with substitution policy and DAW flags
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate each rule | 2. Attempt substitution | 3. Verify outcome
Test_Data: R1 DAW=Y -> block; R2 DAW=N generic-available -> allow; R3 no-generic -> n/a; R4 controlled -> require extra auth; R5 narrow-therapeutic-index -> warn
Expected_Result: Substitution outcomes match policy table per DAW, generic availability, controlled status, and NTI flag

Title: Dispense blocked when order is discontinued before fill
Preconditions: Order discontinued prior to any dispense
Test_Steps: 1. Discontinue the order | 2. Open dispense queue | 3. Attempt to dispense
Expected_Result: Dispense is blocked for a discontinued/stopped order; message indicates order is no longer active; nothing dispensed

Title: Concurrent revise by two clinicians is handled without lost update
Preconditions: Two sessions open the same active order
Test_Steps: 1. Clinician A revises dose and saves | 2. Clinician B (stale view) revises and saves
Test_Data: A sets 250mg; B sets 100mg from stale state
Expected_Result: Optimistic locking or chain validation prevents lost update; B is notified the order changed and must reload; no orphaned/conflicting active orders

Title: Drug search with typo/partial term returns relevant suggestions
Preconditions: Drug dictionary populated
Test_Steps: 1. Type partial/misspelled drug name | 2. Inspect suggestions
Expected_Result: Search returns close matches/synonyms; selecting a suggestion populates the coded drug; empty search handled gracefully

Title: Inactive/retired drug concept is excluded from order search
Preconditions: A drug concept is retired in dictionary
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the retired drug name | 2. Inspect results
Expected_Result: Retired/inactive drugs are not selectable for new orders; only active formulations returned

Title: Dispensed medication generates a charge item linked to the dispense
Preconditions: Billing/cashier module enabled; dispense completed
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a dispense | 2. Open billing for the patient | 3. Verify charge
Expected_Result: A billable charge item is created for the dispensed product with correct quantity and price linkage to the dispense event

Title: Apply an order set/regimen template creates multiple orders atomically
Preconditions: Order set/regimen template defined; clinician with active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Select a regimen order set | 2. Review included drugs | 3. Apply | 4. Verify orders
Expected_Result: All component drug orders are created together; if one fails validation the set application reports it; no partial silent application

Title: Discontinued order can be reordered (renewed) as a fresh order
Preconditions: Order previously discontinued
Test_Steps: 1. Open discontinued order history | 2. Choose reorder/renew | 3. Confirm dosing | 4. Save
Expected_Result: A new active order is created copying prior dosing; original remains discontinued in history; new order has its own lifecycle

Title: Decimal/fractional dose precision is preserved
Preconditions: Drug supports fractional dosing
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug with fractional dose | 2. Save | 3. Reopen and verify
Expected_Result: Fractional dose precision is stored and displayed exactly without rounding errors

Title: Pairwise dispense scenarios across status and substitution
Preconditions: Dispensing module enabled; varied order states
Test_Steps: 1. Execute each pairwise combo | 2. Verify outcome
Test_Data: Pairs: active+full+no-sub=ok; active+partial+generic-sub=ok; DAW+full+sub-attempt=block; discontinued+any=block; refill+partial=track
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the correct dispense outcome and audit entry; invalid combos are blocked
